message:
feat(cli): integrate OutputContext into CLI command dispatch

beads_rust-11n3: All 37 CLI commands now receive &OutputContext
parameter for consistent output handling.

Changes:
- Fixed syntax error in sqlite.rs (orphaned test outside module)
- Updated query.rs to pass OutputContext through to list::execute
- Added OutputContext to init.rs test functions
- Added OutputContext to repro test files
- Fixed clippy: renamed _ctx to ctx in query.rs
- Added allow(too_many_lines) to main() dispatch

All output/init tests pass. cargo check and clippy pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"beads_rust-11n3","title":"Integrate OutputContext into CLI command dispatch","description":"# Task: Integrate OutputContext into CLI Dispatch

## What to Do
Modify the CLI entry point to create OutputContext from global args and pass it to all command handlers.

## Files to Modify

### 1. `src/main.rs` or `src/cli/mod.rs`
Where commands are dispatched, create OutputContext and pass it down.

## Changes Required

### Before (current pattern):
```rust
fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let cli = Cli::parse();
    
    match cli.command {
        Commands::List(args) => commands::list::run(args, &storage)?,
        Commands::Show(args) => commands::show::run(args, &storage)?,
        // ...
    }
}
```

### After (with OutputContext):
```rust
use crate::output::OutputContext;

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    let cli = Cli::parse();
    
    // Create output context from global flags
    let ctx = OutputContext::new(
        cli.json,
        cli.quiet,
        cli.no_color,
    );
    
    match cli.command {
        Commands::List(args) => commands::list::run(args, &storage, &ctx)?,
        Commands::Show(args) => commands::show::run(args, &storage, &ctx)?,
        // ... pass &ctx to all command handlers
    }
}
```

## Global Flags to Wire
Ensure these global flags exist in CLI definition and are passed to OutputContext:
- `--json` (bool): Machine-readable JSON output
- `--quiet` / `-q` (bool): Suppress non-essential output
- `--no-color` (bool): Disable ANSI color codes

## Command Handler Signature Change
Each command handler signature changes from:
```rust
pub fn run(args: ListArgs, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<()>
```
to:
```rust
pub fn run(args: ListArgs, storage: &SqliteStorage, ctx: &OutputContext) -> Result<()>
```

## Implementation Strategy
1. Add OutputContext parameter to ALL command handlers (even if not used yet)
2. Update match arms in dispatch to pass &ctx
3. Commands can ignore ctx initially and keep using println!
4. Commands migrated incrementally to use ctx methods

## Why Pass by Reference?
- OutputContext doesn't need to be mutated by commands
- Avoids ownership complexity
- Enables potential future caching in OutputContext

## Testing
After this change:
- All existing tests should still pass (commands ignore ctx initially)
- Running any command should work as before
- --json, --quiet, --no-color flags should be recognized

## Notes
- This is a BREAKING CHANGE to command handler signatures
- All 37 command handlers must be updated in this task
- Actual migration to use ctx happens in later phases","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:27:51.456568985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:28:13.016099393Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-11n3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-38mz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:28:10.544220130Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-11n3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-nh5h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:28:13.016050731Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-126","title":"epic status + close-eligible Commands","description":"# epic status + close-eligible Commands (optional)

## Purpose
Classic helper for epics: show closure eligibility and optionally close eligible epics.

## CLI
```
br epic status [--eligible-only]
br epic close-eligible [--dry-run]
```

## Behavior
- Epic eligibility: epic is open/in_progress and **all children** (parent-child deps) are closed or tombstone.
- `epic status`: returns `EpicStatus[]` objects with counts and eligibility.
- `epic close-eligible`:
  - `--dry-run`: returns same as `epic status`.
  - normal: closes all eligible epics and returns `{ "closed": [ids], "count": N }`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Eligibility logic matches bd.
- JSON outputs match classic shapes.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:47.705593155Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T06:26:08.678356297Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T06:26:08.678356297Z","close_reason":"Implemented epic status + close-eligible commands","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-135","title":"Implement br update self-update command","description":"# Self-Update Command Implementation

## Purpose
Enable br to update itself to the latest version with a simple command, using cryptographic verification for security.

## Technical Requirements

### Commands
```bash
br update              # Check and install latest version
br update --check      # Check only, don't install
br update --force      # Force reinstall current version
br update --version X  # Install specific version
```

### Dependencies
```toml
[dependencies]
self_update = { version = "0.39", features = ["rustls"], default-features = false }
```

### Implementation
```rust
use self_update::backends::github;
use self_update::cargo_crate_version;

fn update_self(check_only: bool, force: bool) -> Result<()> {
    let status = github::Update::configure()
        .repo_owner("Dicklesworthstone")
        .repo_name("beads_rust")
        .bin_name("br")
        .show_download_progress(true)
        .current_version(cargo_crate_version\!())
        .build()?
        .update()?;
    
    match status {
        Status::UpToDate(v) => println\!("Already at latest version: {}", v),
        Status::Updated(v) => println\!("Updated to version: {}", v),
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Security Features
- SHA256 checksum verification
- Download over HTTPS only (rustls)
- Atomic binary replacement
- Verify before delete old binary

### Output
```
$ br update
Checking for updates...
Current version: 0.1.0
Latest version:  0.2.0

Downloading br v0.2.0...
[████████████████████████████████] 100%

Verifying checksum...
Installing...
✓ Updated br from 0.1.0 to 0.2.0

$ br update --check
Current version: 0.2.0
Latest version:  0.2.0
✓ Already up to date
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `br update` downloads and installs latest
- [ ] `br update --check` only checks
- [ ] Checksum verification works
- [ ] Progress bar during download
- [ ] Atomic replacement (no partial updates)
- [ ] Works on Linux, macOS, Windows

## Dependencies
- CI/CD Pipeline for releases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"StormyBarn","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:29.716863171Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T01:38:06.301596308Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T01:38:06.301596308Z","close_reason":"Self-update implemented as  with --check/--force/--version/dry-run; docs and CLI args present in src/cli/commands/upgrade.rs and CLI_REFERENCE","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-149j","title":"Create JSON baseline fixtures for backward compatibility","description":"## Task: Create JSON Baseline Fixtures

### CRITICAL: Why This Matters
Before making ANY rich output changes, we MUST capture the current JSON output from all commands. This baseline allows us to verify that JSON output remains **byte-identical** after integration.

### When to Run
**BEFORE starting Phase 1** - This is a prerequisite for all other work.

### File Location
`tests/fixtures/json_baseline/`

### Fixtures to Create

#### 1. List Command Fixtures
```bash
# Create test workspace
br init --prefix fix
br create "Test issue 1" --type task --priority 1
br create "Test bug" --type bug --priority 0
br create "Test feature" --type feature --priority 2
br close $(br list --json | jq -r '.[2].id')

# Capture baselines
br list --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/list_all.json
br list --json --all > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/list_with_closed.json
```

#### 2. Show Command Fixtures
```bash
ISSUE_ID=$(br list --json | jq -r '.[0].id')
br show "$ISSUE_ID" --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/show_single.json
br show $(br list --json | jq -r '.[].id' | head -2 | tr '\n' ' ') --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/show_multiple.json
```

#### 3. Ready/Blocked Command Fixtures
```bash
br ready --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/ready.json
br blocked --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/blocked.json
```

#### 4. Stats Command Fixtures
```bash
br stats --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/stats.json
```

#### 5. Search Command Fixtures
```bash
br search "test" --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/search.json
```

#### 6. Dep Command Fixtures
```bash
# Add some dependencies first
ID1=$(br list --json | jq -r '.[0].id')
ID2=$(br list --json | jq -r '.[1].id')
br dep add "$ID1" "$ID2"

br dep list "$ID1" --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/dep_list.json
br dep tree "$ID1" --json > tests/fixtures/json_baseline/dep_tree.json
```

### Automation Script

Create `scripts/generate_json_baseline.sh`:

```bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_FILE="/tmp/br_baseline_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
exec > >(tee -a "$LOG_FILE") 2>&1

log() { echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] $*"; }
log_section() { echo ""; log "═══════════════════════════════════════"; log "$*"; }

FIXTURE_DIR="tests/fixtures/json_baseline"
mkdir -p "$FIXTURE_DIR"

log_section "Creating JSON Baseline Fixtures"
log "Output directory: $FIXTURE_DIR"
log "br version: $(br version --json | jq -r '.version')"

# Create temp workspace
TESTDIR=$(mktemp -d)
cd "$TESTDIR"
trap 'rm -rf "$TESTDIR"' EXIT

log_section "Setup: Create test workspace"
br init --prefix fix
br create "Test task 1" --type task --priority 1
br create "Test bug" --type bug --priority 0
br create "Test feature" --type feature --priority 2 --description "A feature with description"
br create "Closed task" --type task
br close $(br list --json | jq -r '.[-1].id')

# Add dependencies
ID1=$(br list --json | jq -r '.[0].id')
ID2=$(br list --json | jq -r '.[1].id')
br dep add "$ID1" "$ID2"

log_section "Capturing: List commands"
br list --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/list.json"
br list --all --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/list_all.json"
log "Captured list fixtures"

log_section "Capturing: Show command"
br show "$ID1" --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/show.json"
log "Captured show fixture"

log_section "Capturing: Ready/Blocked"
br ready --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/ready.json"
br blocked --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/blocked.json"
log "Captured ready/blocked fixtures"

log_section "Capturing: Stats"
br stats --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/stats.json"
log "Captured stats fixture"

log_section "Capturing: Search"
br search "test" --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/search.json" || true
log "Captured search fixture"

log_section "Capturing: Dependencies"
br dep list "$ID1" --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/dep_list.json"
br dep tree "$ID1" --json > "$FIXTURE_DIR/dep_tree.json" || true
log "Captured dependency fixtures"

log_section "Baseline Generation Complete"
log "Fixtures saved to: $FIXTURE_DIR"
ls -la "$FIXTURE_DIR"
log "Log file: $LOG_FILE"
```

### Verification

After capturing, verify all fixtures are valid JSON:
```bash
for f in tests/fixtures/json_baseline/*.json; do
    jq -e '.' "$f" > /dev/null && echo "✓ $f" || echo "✗ INVALID: $f"
done
```

### Usage in Tests

```rust
// tests/format/json_compat_tests.rs

fn load_baseline(name: &str) -> serde_json::Value {
    let path = format\!("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/{}.json", name);
    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path)
        .expect(&format\!("Baseline fixture not found: {}", path));
    serde_json::from_str(&content).expect("Invalid JSON in baseline")
}

#[test]
fn test_list_json_matches_baseline() {
    let baseline = load_baseline("list");
    let current = run_br(&["list", "--json"]);
    
    // Compare structure (ignore timestamps)
    assert_json_structure_matches(&baseline, &current);
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All JSON-producing commands have baseline fixtures
- [ ] All fixtures are valid JSON (jq validates)
- [ ] Automation script is executable and working
- [ ] Fixtures committed to repository
- [ ] Test helpers for loading baselines","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:02:54.098808478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:19:58.286770697Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:19:58.286698191Z","close_reason":"Created JSON baseline fixtures for all commands with --json flag. 16 valid fixtures in tests/fixtures/json_baseline/. Added test helper module tests/common/json_baseline.rs. Generator script at scripts/generate_json_baseline.sh.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-149j","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-6llm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:03:05.139375190Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+            status_in_progress: Style::new().color(color("yellow")).bold(),
+            status_blocked: Style::new().color(color("red")),
+            status_deferred: Style::new().color(color("blue")).dim(),
+            status_closed: Style::new().color(color("bright_black")),
+
+            priority_critical: Style::new().color(color("red")).bold(),
+            priority_high: Style::new().color(color("red")),
+            priority_medium: Style::new().color(color("yellow")),
+            priority_low: Style::new().color(color("green")),
+            priority_backlog: Style::new().color(color("bright_black")),
+
+            type_task: Style::new().color(color("blue")),
+            type_bug: Style::new().color(color("red")),
+            type_feature: Style::new().color(color("green")),
+            type_epic: Style::new().color(color("magenta")).bold(),
+            type_chore: Style::new().color(color("bright_black")),
+            type_docs: Style::new().color(color("cyan")),
+            type_question: Style::new().color(color("yellow")),
+
+            table_header: Style::new().bold(),
+            table_border: Style::new().color(color("bright_black")),
+            panel_title: Style::new().bold(),
+            panel_border: Style::new().color(color("bright_black")),
+            section: Style::new().color(color("cyan")).bold(),
+            label: Style::new().color(color("cyan")).dim(),
+            timestamp: Style::new().color(color("bright_black")),
+            username: Style::new().color(color("green")),
+            comment: Style::new().italic(),
+
+            box_style: &ROUNDED,
         }
     }
 }
 
 impl Theme {
-    pub fn status_style(&self, _status: &Status) -> Style {
-        Style::default()
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn status_style(&self, status: &Status) -> Style {
+        match status {
+            Status::Open => self.status_open.clone(),
+            Status::InProgress => self.status_in_progress.clone(),
+            Status::Blocked => self.status_blocked.clone(),
+            Status::Deferred => self.status_deferred.clone(),
+            Status::Closed => self.status_closed.clone(),
+            Status::Tombstone | Status::Custom(_) => self.muted.clone(),
+            Status::Pinned => self.highlight.clone(),
+        }
     }
 
-    pub fn priority_style(&self, _priority: Priority) -> Style {
-        Style::default()
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn priority_style(&self, priority: Priority) -> Style {
+        match priority.0 {
+            0 => self.priority_critical.clone(),
+            1 => self.priority_high.clone(),
+            2 => self.priority_medium.clone(),
+            3 => self.priority_low.clone(),
+            _ => self.priority_backlog.clone(),
+        }
     }
 
-    pub fn type_style(&self, _issue_type: &IssueType) -> Style {
-        Style::default()
+    #[must_use]
+    pub fn type_style(&self, issue_type: &IssueType) -> Style {
+        match issue_type {
+            IssueType::Task => self.type_task.clone(),
+            IssueType::Bug => self.type_bug.clone(),
+            IssueType::Feature => self.type_feature.clone(),
+            IssueType::Epic => self.type_epic.clone(),
+            IssueType::Chore => self.type_chore.clone(),
+            IssueType::Docs => self.type_docs.clone(),
+            IssueType::Question => self.type_question.clone(),
+            IssueType::Custom(_) => self.muted.clone(),
+        }
     }
 }
